content.problemStatement:
A circular binary string is called good if it contains at least one character '1' and there is an integer ddd such that for each character '1', the distance to the nearest character '1' clockwise is ddd.

You are given a circular binary string SSS with length NNN. You may choose some characters in this string and flip them (i.e. change '1' to '0' or vice versa). Convert the given string into a good string by flipping the smallest possible number of characters.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a single string SSS with length NNN.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ― the minimum number of flips required to transform the initial string into a good string.
